# Implement my Finetuning assignment:

* This assignment aims to provide hands-on experience with instruction tuning and preference fine-tuning of a small base model (TinyLlama-1.1B, Qwen3-0.6B, etc.) for instruction following and reasoning tasks. You will complete a fine-tuning pipeline with comparative analysis.
* Select any dataset which is beginner friendly and satisfies assignment requirement.
* We will implement in phases (one goal achieved at a time), since this is requires me training on colab, i would use your files and run on colab and then step by step we will complete the assignment. 
* maintain documentation in parallel to implementation incrementally in a .md so final report is being made in parallel.
# LLM Fine-Tuning (Standard Dialogue/QA)
 Preliminary:
• Select a base model.
• Generate a manual test set of prompts/questions with reference response (you
may use ChatGPT, Mistral, DeepSeek to generate reference response).
• Ask base models these questions and record its response and the corresponding
BLEU score +BERTScore. Note: Your ChatGPT/Clause/Gemini response would
serve as the gold answer.
## Standard Pipeline (SFT → DPO)
1. Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT)
o Dataset: Any instruction–response dataset from HuggingFace (not used in
class).
o Perform five fine-tuning trials total. In each trial, vary both LoRA parameters
(rank, target modules) and training arguments (learning rate, batch size,
epochs). Collectively, your five trials should explore a wide range of
configurations.
o Selecting the Best SFT Model: After completing your 5 SFT trials, select one to
proceed to Step 2 using:
1. BLEU + BERTScore on the 10 prompts that you tested with baseline
model earlier.
2. If scores turn out to be similar, then use Lower validation loss as the
tie breaker.
o Report BLEU + BERTScore for each trial and clearly state which trial was
selected as best and why.
2. Preference Fine-Tuning (DPO)
o Dataset: Any preference dataset (chosen, rejected responses).
Page 2 of 4
o Fine-tune the best SFT model from step 1 using DPO + LoRA.
o Report 5 DPO trials (vary beta, LR, batch size, epochs).
o Select the best SFT+DPO model using:
1. BLEU + BERTScore on the 10 prompts that you tested with baseline
model earlier.
2. If scores turn out to be similar then use Lower validation loss as the
tie breaker.
o Report BLEU + BERTScore for each trial and clearly state which trial was
selected as best and why.

# Deliverables
1. Submit clean and well-documented Python code notebook(s) that were used for
experimentation via GitHub or as a zipped folder.
2. A well-organized report, either in the docx or PDF format.

# Report Guidelines
Organize your report into clearly defined sections. In your report, include discussion on:
1. Platform Details: Specify the platform used for experimentation (e.g., local machine,
Kaggle, Colab). If multiple platforms were used, clarify where each stage was
executed.
2. Data Details: Clearly state the sources of the datasets, including the size and number
of samples used in the dataset. You may use subsets of these datasets as appropriate
for your compute budget but justify your choices in the report. Describe the datasets
selection and preprocessing steps
3. Experimentation, Analysis, and Insight: Clearly document the experimental setup and
results, highlighting insights gained from multiple trials.
o Model and tokenizer choice
o Evaluation metrics
o The impact of different LoRA and training configurations on the responses.
o Differences in behavior between:
▪ Base model
▪ Instruction-tuned model
▪ Preference-tuned model (after DPO or GRPO)
o List parameters of the best-performing models (e.g., LoRA rank, DPO beta,
learning rate).
o Output quality: include examples that illustrate improvement or degradation
Page 4 of 4
o Resource usage and training time
o Strengths and weaknesses of SFT DPO and GRPO (for respective
options/experimetns), the impact of hyperparameter choices, and scenarios
where each approach excels.
o Common failure cases or unexpected behaviors.
4. Reproducibility: Your report must provide enough detail to enable others to replicate
your work. Include any information that is critical for reproduction, such as
preprocessing steps, system configuration, or model fine-tuning techniques.
Additional Instructions:
• Application Development (Optional): If you have developed a working application
based on your system, include screenshots and relevant details in an appendix at the
end of your report.
• Figures and Tables: Ensure that all figures and tables are properly numbered and cited
in the text. Avoid vague references like “the figure below”; instead, use precise
citations such as “Table 1 shows…” or “As shown in Figure 7…”.
• References: If you have used external resources, such as blogs or GitHub repositories,
ensure they are appropriately cited. Include a reference section before the appendix
to acknowledge all sources and avoid any potential issues of plagiarism. Proper
citation is a key part of your academic and professional training.
• Submission File Name: The file name should be as per the group members name and
don’t name it Assignment1 or Project1. So, if there are two group members, Aamna
and Zaid, then name it Aamna_Zaid.docx.

# LLM Fine-Tuning Assignment — Implementation Plan

## Context
This assignment implements a full SFT → DPO fine-tuning pipeline on TinyLlama-1.1B. The project is run on Google Colab in phases (baseline → SFT → DPO), with a parallel report maintained in Markdown. The directory `E:\Dev\NLP Finetuning project` is empty — everything is created from scratch.

---

## Chosen Stack

{
 "nbformat": 4,
 "nbformat_minor": 0,
 "metadata": {
  "colab": {
   "provenance": [],
   "gpuType": "T4"
  },
  "kernelspec": {
   "name": "python3",
   "display_name": "Python 3"
  },
  "language_info": {
   "name": "python"
  },
  "accelerator": "GPU"
 },
 "cells": [
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "# Phase 1 — Baseline Evaluation\n",
    "**Model:** `TinyLlama/TinyLlama-1.1B-intermediate-step-1431k-3T` (raw base, no fine-tuning)\n",
    "\n",
    "**Goal:** Establish baseline BLEU and BERTScore on 10 manually crafted test prompts before any fine-tuning.\n",
    "\n",
    "> **Before running:** Open `test_prompts.json`, paste a GPT-4o / Mistral / DeepSeek response into each `\"reference\"` field, then upload the file to this Colab session (or mount Google Drive and update `PROMPTS_PATH` below)."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 1: Install dependencies ──────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "!pip install -q transformers==4.44.2 bitsandbytes==0.43.3 accelerate==0.34.2 \\\n",
    "    evaluate==0.4.3 bert-score==0.3.13 sacrebleu==2.4.3"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 2: Mount Google Drive (optional — skip if uploading files directly) ──\n",
    "from google.colab import drive\n",
    "drive.mount('/content/drive')"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 3: Imports ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "import json\n",
    "import re\n",
    "import time\n",
    "import torch\n",
    "import pandas as pd\n",
    "import evaluate\n",
    "from bert_score import score as bert_score_fn\n",
    "from transformers import AutoTokenizer, AutoModelForCausalLM, BitsAndBytesConfig\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"CUDA available: {torch.cuda.is_available()}\")\n",
    "if torch.cuda.is_available():\n",
    "    print(f\"GPU: {torch.cuda.get_device_name(0)}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"VRAM: {torch.cuda.get_device_properties(0).total_memory / 1e9:.1f} GB\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 4: Config ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "MODEL_ID    = \"TinyLlama/TinyLlama-1.1B-intermediate-step-1431k-3T\"\n",
    "PROMPTS_PATH = \"test_prompts.json\"   # change to Drive path if needed\n",
    "RESULTS_PATH = \"/content/drive/MyDrive/baseline_results.csv\"  # where to save\n",
    "MAX_NEW_TOKENS = 200\n",
    "TEMPERATURE    = 0.7\n",
    "TOP_P          = 0.9"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 5: Load test prompts ──────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "with open(PROMPTS_PATH) as f:\n",
    "    data = json.load(f)\n",
    "\n",
    "prompts    = [p[\"prompt\"]    for p in data[\"prompts\"]]\n",
    "references = [p[\"reference\"] for p in data[\"prompts\"]]\n",
    "types      = [p[\"type\"]      for p in data[\"prompts\"]]\n",
    "\n",
    "empty = [i+1 for i, r in enumerate(references) if not r.strip()]\n",
    "if empty:\n",
    "    raise ValueError(\n",
    "        f\"Reference answers missing for prompt IDs: {empty}.\\n\"\n",
    "        \"Please fill in test_prompts.json before running evaluation.\"\n",
    "    )\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"Loaded {len(prompts)} prompts — all references present.\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 6: Load model in 4-bit NF4 ───────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "bnb_config = BitsAndBytesConfig(\n",
    "    load_in_4bit=True,\n",
    "    bnb_4bit_quant_type=\"nf4\",\n",
    "    bnb_4bit_use_double_quant=True,\n",
    "    bnb_4bit_compute_dtype=torch.float16,\n",
    ")\n",
    "\n",
    "tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(MODEL_ID)\n",
    "tokenizer.pad_token = tokenizer.eos_token\n",
    "tokenizer.padding_side = \"left\"\n",
    "\n",
    "model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(\n",
    "    MODEL_ID,\n",
    "    quantization_config=bnb_config,\n",
    "    device_map=\"auto\",\n",
    ")\n",
    "model.eval()\n",
    "print(\"Model loaded.\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 7: Generation helper ──────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "def format_prompt(instruction: str) -> str:\n",
    "    \"\"\"Alpaca-style prompt format.\"\"\"\n",
    "    return (\n",
    "        \"Below is an instruction that describes a task. \"\n",
    "        \"Write a response that appropriately completes the request.\\n\\n\"\n",
    "        f\"### Instruction:\\n{instruction}\\n\\n### Response:\\n\"\n",
    "    )\n",
    "\n",
    "@torch.inference_mode()\n",
    "def generate(instruction: str) -> str:\n",
    "    prompt = format_prompt(instruction)\n",
    "    inputs = tokenizer(prompt, return_tensors=\"pt\").to(model.device)\n",
    "    input_len = inputs[\"input_ids\"].shape[1]\n",
    "    outputs = model.generate(\n",
    "        **inputs,\n",
    "        max_new_tokens=MAX_NEW_TOKENS,\n",
    "        do_sample=True,\n",
    "        temperature=TEMPERATURE,\n",
    "        top_p=TOP_P,\n",
    "        pad_token_id=tokenizer.eos_token_id,\n",
    "    )\n",
    "    # Decode only the newly generated tokens\n",
    "    new_tokens = outputs[0][input_len:]\n",
    "    return tokenizer.decode(new_tokens, skip_special_tokens=True).strip()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 8: Generate baseline responses ───────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "print(\"Generating baseline responses...\")\n",
    "generated = []\n",
    "for i, prompt in enumerate(prompts):\n",
    "    t0 = time.time()\n",
    "    response = generate(prompt)\n",
    "    elapsed = time.time() - t0\n",
    "    generated.append(response)\n",
    "    print(f\"[{i+1:02d}/10] ({elapsed:.1f}s) {prompt[:60]}...\")\n",
    "    print(f\"   → {response[:120]}...\\n\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 9: Compute BLEU scores ────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "sacrebleu = evaluate.load(\"sacrebleu\")\n",
    "\n",
    "bleu_scores = []\n",
    "for gen, ref in zip(generated, references):\n",
    "    result = sacrebleu.compute(\n",
    "        predictions=[gen],\n",
    "        references=[[ref]],\n",
    "    )\n",
    "    bleu_scores.append(round(result[\"score\"], 4))\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"BLEU scores:\", bleu_scores)\n",
    "print(f\"Mean BLEU: {sum(bleu_scores)/len(bleu_scores):.4f}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 10: Compute BERTScore ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "P, R, F1 = bert_score_fn(\n",
    "    generated,\n",
    "    references,\n",
    "    lang=\"en\",\n",
    "    rescale_with_baseline=True,\n",
    "    verbose=True,\n",
    ")\n",
    "\n",
    "bert_f1 = [round(f.item(), 4) for f in F1]\n",
    "bert_p  = [round(p.item(), 4) for p in P]\n",
    "bert_r  = [round(r.item(), 4) for r in R]\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"BERTScore F1:\", bert_f1)\n",
    "print(f\"Mean BERTScore F1: {sum(bert_f1)/len(bert_f1):.4f}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 11: Results table ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "df = pd.DataFrame({\n",
    "    \"id\":           range(1, 11),\n",
    "    \"type\":         types,\n",
    "    \"prompt\":       prompts,\n",
    "    \"reference\":    references,\n",
    "    \"generated\":    generated,\n",
    "    \"bleu\":         bleu_scores,\n",
    "    \"bert_p\":       bert_p,\n",
    "    \"bert_r\":       bert_r,\n",
    "    \"bert_f1\":      bert_f1,\n",
    "})\n",
    "\n",
    "pd.set_option(\"display.max_colwidth\", 80)\n",
    "print(df[[\"id\",\"type\",\"bleu\",\"bert_f1\"]].to_string(index=False))\n",
    "print(f\"\\nMean BLEU:         {df['bleu'].mean():.4f}\")\n",
    "print(f\"Mean BERTScore F1: {df['bert_f1'].mean():.4f}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 12: Save results to Google Drive ─────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "df.to_csv(RESULTS_PATH, index=False)\n",
    "print(f\"Results saved to {RESULTS_PATH}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# Also print full generated outputs for report\n",
    "print(\"\\n\" + \"=\"*80)\n",
    "print(\"FULL GENERATED OUTPUTS (for report)\")\n",
    "print(\"=\"*80)\n",
    "for i, row in df.iterrows():\n",
    "    print(f\"\\n[{row['id']}] {row['type'].upper()}: {row['prompt']}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"Generated: {row['generated']}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"BLEU: {row['bleu']}  |  BERTScore F1: {row['bert_f1']}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Summary\n",
    "\n",
    "Copy the mean BLEU and mean BERTScore F1 into **Section 3 (Baseline Results)** of `report.md`.\n",
    "\n",
    "**Next step:** Run `01_sft_training.ipynb` to fine-tune TinyLlama on `tatsu-lab/alpaca`."
   ]
  }
 ]
}

    "df.to_csv(RESULTS_PATH, index=False)\n",
    "print(f\"Results saved to {RESULTS_PATH}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# Also print full generated outputs for report\n",
    "print(\"\\n\" + \"=\"*80)\n",
    "print(\"FULL GENERATED OUTPUTS (for report)\")\n",
    "print(\"=\"*80)\n",
    "for i, row in df.iterrows():\n",
    "    print(f\"\\n[{row['id']}] {row['type'].upper()}: {row['prompt']}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"Generated: {row['generated']}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"BLEU: {row['bleu']}  |  BERTScore F1: {row['bert_f1']}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Summary\n",
    "\n",
    "Copy the mean BLEU and mean BERTScore F1 into **Section 3 (Baseline Results)** of `report.md`.\n",
    "\n",
    "**Next step:** Run `01_sft_training.ipynb` to fine-tune TinyLlama on `tatsu-lab/alpaca`."
   ]
  }
 ]
}

{

 },
 "cells": [
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "# Phase 2 — Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) — 5 Trials\n",
    "\n",
    "**Base model:** `TinyLlama/TinyLlama-1.1B-intermediate-step-1431k-3T`  \n",
    "**Dataset:** `tatsu-lab/alpaca` (10,000 samples subset)  \n",
    "**Method:** LoRA via PEFT + TRL SFTTrainer + 4-bit NF4 quantization\n",
    "\n",
    "**Before running:**\n",
    "1. Mount Google Drive (Cell 2).\n",
    "2. Upload `test_prompts.json` (with filled reference answers) to the session or Drive.\n",
    "3. Update `PROMPTS_PATH` and `DRIVE_OUTPUT_DIR` in Cell 4 if needed.\n",
    "4. Runtime → Change runtime type → **T4 GPU**."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 1: Install dependencies ──────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "!pip install -q transformers==4.44.2 peft==0.12.0 trl==0.9.6 \\\n",
    "    bitsandbytes==0.43.3 accelerate==0.34.2 datasets==2.21.0 \\\n",
    "    evaluate==0.4.3 bert-score==0.3.13 sacrebleu==2.4.3"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 2: Mount Google Drive ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "from google.colab import drive\n",
    "drive.mount('/content/drive')"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 3: Imports ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "import gc\n",
    "import json\n",
    "import os\n",
    "import time\n",
    "\n",
    "import evaluate\n",
    "import pandas as pd\n",
    "import torch\n",
    "from bert_score import score as bert_score_fn\n",
    "from datasets import load_dataset\n",
    "from peft import LoraConfig, get_peft_model, TaskType\n",
    "from transformers import (\n",
    "    AutoModelForCausalLM,\n",
    "    AutoTokenizer,\n",
    "    BitsAndBytesConfig,\n",
    "    TrainingArguments,\n",
    ")\n",
    "from trl import SFTTrainer, DataCollatorForCompletionOnlyLM\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"CUDA: {torch.cuda.is_available()}\")\n",
    "if torch.cuda.is_available():\n",
    "    print(f\"GPU: {torch.cuda.get_device_name(0)}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 4: Config ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "MODEL_ID          = \"TinyLlama/TinyLlama-1.1B-intermediate-step-1431k-3T\"\n",
    "PROMPTS_PATH      = \"test_prompts.json\"         # upload to session or set Drive path\n",
    "DRIVE_OUTPUT_DIR  = \"/content/drive/MyDrive/sft_trials\"   # adapters saved here\n",
    "SFT_RESULTS_PATH  = \"/content/drive/MyDrive/sft_results.csv\"\n",
    "DATASET_N_TRAIN   = 9000     # 90% of 10k subset\n",
    "DATASET_N_VAL     = 1000     # 10% of 10k subset\n",
    "MAX_SEQ_LENGTH    = 512\n",
    "MAX_NEW_TOKENS    = 200\n",
    "TEMPERATURE       = 0.7\n",
    "TOP_P             = 0.9\n",
    "\n",
    "os.makedirs(DRIVE_OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 5: Load and inspect alpaca dataset ────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "raw = load_dataset(\"tatsu-lab/alpaca\", split=\"train\")\n",
    "raw = raw.shuffle(seed=42).select(range(10000))\n",
    "split = raw.train_test_split(test_size=0.1, seed=42)\n",
    "train_ds = split[\"train\"]\n",
    "val_ds   = split[\"test\"]\n",
    "print(f\"Train: {len(train_ds)}  |  Val: {len(val_ds)}\")\n",
    "print(\"Sample:\", train_ds[0])"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 6: Dataset formatting ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "RESPONSE_TEMPLATE = \"### Response:\\n\"\n",
    "\n",
    "def format_alpaca(example):\n",
    "    if example[\"input\"].strip():\n",
    "        text = (\n",
    "            \"Below is an instruction that describes a task, paired with an input \"\n",
    "            \"that provides further context. Write a response that appropriately \"\n",
    "            \"completes the request.\\n\\n\"\n",
    "            f\"### Instruction:\\n{example['instruction']}\\n\\n\"\n",
    "            f\"### Input:\\n{example['input']}\\n\\n\"\n",
    "            f\"### Response:\\n{example['output']}\"\n",
    "        )\n",
    "    else:\n",
    "        text = (\n",
    "            \"Below is an instruction that describes a task. Write a response that \"\n",
    "            \"appropriately completes the request.\\n\\n\"\n",
    "            f\"### Instruction:\\n{example['instruction']}\\n\\n\"\n",
    "            f\"### Response:\\n{example['output']}\"\n",
    "        )\n",
    "    return {\"text\": text}\n",
    "\n",
    "train_ds = train_ds.map(format_alpaca, remove_columns=train_ds.column_names)\n",
    "val_ds   = val_ds.map(format_alpaca,   remove_columns=val_ds.column_names)\n",
    "print(\"Formatted. Sample:\\n\", train_ds[0][\"text\"][:300])"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 7: Load test prompts for evaluation ───────────────────────────────────\n",
    "with open(PROMPTS_PATH) as f:\n",
    "    data = json.load(f)\n",
    "\n",
    "test_prompts    = [p[\"prompt\"]    for p in data[\"prompts\"]]\n",
    "test_references = [p[\"reference\"] for p in data[\"prompts\"]]\n",
    "\n",
    "empty = [i+1 for i, r in enumerate(test_references) if not r.strip()]\n",
    "if empty:\n",
    "    raise ValueError(f\"Missing references for prompt IDs: {empty}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "sacrebleu = evaluate.load(\"sacrebleu\")\n",
    "print(f\"Loaded {len(test_prompts)} test prompts.\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 8: Evaluation helper ──────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "def evaluate_model(model, tokenizer, prompts, references):\n",
    "    \"\"\"Run BLEU + BERTScore on 10 test prompts. Returns (mean_bleu, mean_bert_f1, generated_texts).\"\"\"\n",
    "    model.eval()\n",
    "    generated = []\n",
    "    for prompt in prompts:\n",
    "        formatted = (\n",
    "            \"Below is an instruction that describes a task. Write a response that \"\n",
    "            \"appropriately completes the request.\\n\\n\"\n",
    "            f\"### Instruction:\\n{prompt}\\n\\n### Response:\\n\"\n",
    "        )\n",
    "        inputs = tokenizer(formatted, return_tensors=\"pt\").to(model.device)\n",
    "        input_len = inputs[\"input_ids\"].shape[1]\n",
    "        with torch.inference_mode():\n",
    "            out = model.generate(\n",
    "                **inputs,\n",
    "                max_new_tokens=MAX_NEW_TOKENS,\n",
    "                do_sample=True,\n",
    "                temperature=TEMPERATURE,\n",
    "                top_p=TOP_P,\n",
    "                pad_token_id=tokenizer.eos_token_id,\n",
    "            )\n",
    "        text = tokenizer.decode(out[0][input_len:], skip_special_tokens=True).strip()\n",
    "        generated.append(text)\n",
    "\n",
    "    bleu_scores = [\n",
    "        sacrebleu.compute(predictions=[g], references=[[r]])[\"score\"]\n",
    "        for g, r in zip(generated, references)\n",
    "    ]\n",
    "    _, _, F1 = bert_score_fn(generated, references, lang=\"en\",\n",
    "                              rescale_with_baseline=True, verbose=False)\n",
    "    bert_f1 = [f.item() for f in F1]\n",
    "\n",
    "    return round(sum(bleu_scores)/len(bleu_scores), 4), \\\n",
    "           round(sum(bert_f1)/len(bert_f1), 4), \\\n",
    "           generated"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 9: 4-bit BnB config (shared across all trials) ───────────────────────\n",
    "bnb_config = BitsAndBytesConfig(\n",
    "    load_in_4bit=True,\n",
    "    bnb_4bit_quant_type=\"nf4\",\n",
    "    bnb_4bit_use_double_quant=True,\n",
    "    bnb_4bit_compute_dtype=torch.float16,\n",
    ")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 10: Trial configurations ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "# Each config: lora params + training hyperparams\n",
    "TRIAL_CONFIGS = [\n",
    "    {\n",
    "        \"name\": \"T1\",\n",
    "        \"desc\": \"Minimal LoRA (r=4), 1 epoch\",\n",
    "        \"lora\": {\n",
    "            \"r\": 4,\n",
    "            \"lora_alpha\": 8,\n",
    "            \"target_modules\": [\"q_proj\", \"v_proj\"],\n",
    "            \"lora_dropout\": 0.05,\n",
    "        },\n",
    "        \"train\": {\n",
    "            \"learning_rate\": 2e-4,\n",
    "            \"per_device_train_batch_size\": 4,\n",
    "            \"gradient_accumulation_steps\": 4,\n",
    "            \"num_train_epochs\": 1,\n",
    "        },\n",
    "    },\n",
    "    {\n",
    "        \"name\": \"T2\",\n",
    "        \"desc\": \"r=8, same modules, 2 epochs\",\n",
    "        \"lora\": {\n",
    "            \"r\": 8,\n",
    "            \"lora_alpha\": 16,\n",
    "            \"target_modules\": [\"q_proj\", \"v_proj\"],\n",
    "            \"lora_dropout\": 0.05,\n",
    "        },\n",
    "        \"train\": {\n",
    "            \"learning_rate\": 2e-4,\n",
    "            \"per_device_train_batch_size\": 4,\n",
    "            \"gradient_accumulation_steps\": 4,\n",
    "            \"num_train_epochs\": 2,\n",
    "        },\n",
    "    },\n",
    "    {\n",
    "        \"name\": \"T3\",\n",
    "        \"desc\": \"r=16, all attn modules, 2 epochs\",\n",
    "        \"lora\": {\n",
    "            \"r\": 16,\n",
    "            \"lora_alpha\": 32,\n",
    "            \"target_modules\": [\"q_proj\", \"k_proj\", \"v_proj\", \"o_proj\"],\n",
    "            \"lora_dropout\": 0.05,\n",
    "        },\n",
    "        \"train\": {\n",
    "            \"learning_rate\": 1e-4,\n",
    "            \"per_device_train_batch_size\": 8,\n",
    "            \"gradient_accumulation_steps\": 2,\n",
    "            \"num_train_epochs\": 2,\n",
    "        },\n",
    "    },\n",
    "    {\n",
    "        \"name\": \"T4\",\n",
    "        \"desc\": \"r=32, full LoRA (attn+MLP), low LR\",\n",
    "        \"lora\": {\n",
    "            \"r\": 32,\n",
    "            \"lora_alpha\": 64,\n",
    "            \"target_modules\": [\n",
    "                \"q_proj\", \"k_proj\", \"v_proj\", \"o_proj\",\n",
    "                \"gate_proj\", \"up_proj\", \"down_proj\",\n",
    "            ],\n",
    "            \"lora_dropout\": 0.05,\n",
    "        },\n",
    "        \"train\": {\n",
    "            \"learning_rate\": 5e-5,\n",
    "            \"per_device_train_batch_size\": 4,\n",
    "            \"gradient_accumulation_steps\": 4,\n",
    "            \"num_train_epochs\": 2,\n",
    "        },\n",
    "    },\n",
    "    {\n",
    "        \"name\": \"T5\",\n",
    "        \"desc\": \"r=8, high LR (5e-4), 1 epoch\",\n",
    "        \"lora\": {\n",
    "            \"r\": 8,\n",
    "            \"lora_alpha\": 16,\n",
    "            \"target_modules\": [\"q_proj\", \"v_proj\"],\n",
    "            \"lora_dropout\": 0.05,\n",
    "        },\n",
    "        \"train\": {\n",
    "            \"learning_rate\": 5e-4,\n",
    "            \"per_device_train_batch_size\": 8,\n",
    "            \"gradient_accumulation_steps\": 2,\n",
    "            \"num_train_epochs\": 1,\n",
    "        },\n",
    "    },\n",
    "]"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 11: Training loop (runs all 5 trials sequentially) ───────────────────\n",
    "# Each trial: load fresh base model → apply LoRA → train → eval → save adapter\n",
    "# Estimated time per trial on T4: ~20-40 min depending on config.\n",
    "\n",
    "results = []\n",
    "\n",
    "for cfg in TRIAL_CONFIGS:\n",
    "    trial_name = cfg[\"name\"]\n",
    "    print(f\"\\n{'='*70}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"  TRIAL {trial_name}: {cfg['desc']}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"{'='*70}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── Load tokenizer + fresh base model ─────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(MODEL_ID)\n",
    "    tokenizer.pad_token = tokenizer.eos_token\n",
    "    tokenizer.padding_side = \"right\"  # required for SFT causal LM training\n",
    "\n",
    "    base_model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(\n",
    "        MODEL_ID,\n",
    "        quantization_config=bnb_config,\n",
    "        device_map=\"auto\",\n",
    "    )\n",
    "    base_model.config.use_cache = False\n",
    "    base_model.config.pretraining_tp = 1\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── LoRA config ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    lora_config = LoraConfig(\n",
    "        r=cfg[\"lora\"][\"r\"],\n",
    "        lora_alpha=cfg[\"lora\"][\"lora_alpha\"],\n",
    "        target_modules=cfg[\"lora\"][\"target_modules\"],\n",
    "        lora_dropout=cfg[\"lora\"][\"lora_dropout\"],\n",
    "        bias=\"none\",\n",
    "        task_type=TaskType.CAUSAL_LM,\n",
    "    )\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── Output dir ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    output_dir = f\"/content/sft_{trial_name}\"  # local first, copy to Drive after\n",
    "    drive_dir  = f\"{DRIVE_OUTPUT_DIR}/{trial_name}\"\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── Training arguments ────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    training_args = TrainingArguments(\n",
    "        output_dir=output_dir,\n",
    "        num_train_epochs=cfg[\"train\"][\"num_train_epochs\"],\n",
    "        per_device_train_batch_size=cfg[\"train\"][\"per_device_train_batch_size\"],\n",
    "        gradient_accumulation_steps=cfg[\"train\"][\"gradient_accumulation_steps\"],\n",
    "        per_device_eval_batch_size=4,\n",
    "        learning_rate=cfg[\"train\"][\"learning_rate\"],\n",
    "        warmup_ratio=0.03,\n",
    "        lr_scheduler_type=\"cosine\",\n",
    "        weight_decay=0.01,\n",
    "        fp16=True,\n",
    "        logging_steps=50,\n",
    "        eval_strategy=\"epoch\",\n",
    "        save_strategy=\"epoch\",\n",
    "        load_best_model_at_end=True,\n",
    "        report_to=\"none\",\n",
    "        optim=\"paged_adamw_8bit\",\n",
    "    )\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── SFTTrainer ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    trainer = SFTTrainer(\n",
    "        model=base_model,\n",
    "        train_dataset=train_ds,\n",
    "        eval_dataset=val_ds,\n",
    "        peft_config=lora_config,\n",
    "        dataset_text_field=\"text\",\n",
    "        max_seq_length=MAX_SEQ_LENGTH,\n",
    "        tokenizer=tokenizer,\n",
    "        args=training_args,\n",
    "        packing=False,\n",
    "    )\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── Train ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    t0 = time.time()\n",
    "    train_result = trainer.train()\n",
    "    train_time = time.time() - t0\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── Val loss ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    eval_output = trainer.evaluate()\n",
    "    val_loss = round(eval_output[\"eval_loss\"], 4)\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── Save adapter (local then Drive) ───────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    trainer.model.save_pretrained(output_dir)\n",
    "    tokenizer.save_pretrained(output_dir)\n",
    "    os.makedirs(drive_dir, exist_ok=True)\n",
    "    !cp -r {output_dir}/* {drive_dir}/\n",
    "    print(f\"Adapter saved to {drive_dir}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── Evaluate on test prompts ───────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    print(\"Evaluating on 10 test prompts...\")\n",
    "    tokenizer.padding_side = \"left\"  # switch for generation\n",
    "    mean_bleu, mean_bert_f1, generated_texts = evaluate_model(\n",
    "        trainer.model, tokenizer, test_prompts, test_references\n",
    "    )\n",
    "\n",
    "    results.append({\n",
    "        \"trial\": trial_name,\n",
    "        \"desc\": cfg[\"desc\"],\n",
    "        \"r\": cfg[\"lora\"][\"r\"],\n",
    "        \"lora_alpha\": cfg[\"lora\"][\"lora_alpha\"],\n",
    "        \"target_modules\": \", \".join(cfg[\"lora\"][\"target_modules\"]),\n",
    "        \"lr\": cfg[\"train\"][\"learning_rate\"],\n",
    "        \"batch_size\": cfg[\"train\"][\"per_device_train_batch_size\"],\n",
    "        \"grad_accum\": cfg[\"train\"][\"gradient_accumulation_steps\"],\n",
    "        \"epochs\": cfg[\"train\"][\"num_train_epochs\"],\n",
    "        \"val_loss\": val_loss,\n",
    "        \"mean_bleu\": mean_bleu,\n",
    "        \"mean_bert_f1\": mean_bert_f1,\n",
    "        \"train_time_min\": round(train_time / 60, 1),\n",
    "        \"adapter_path\": drive_dir,\n",
    "        \"generated_texts\": generated_texts,\n",
    "    })\n",
    "\n",
    "    print(f\"\\n  {trial_name} | val_loss={val_loss} | BLEU={mean_bleu} | BERTScore F1={mean_bert_f1} | time={train_time/60:.1f}min\")\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── Free GPU memory before next trial ─────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    del trainer, base_model\n",
    "    gc.collect()\n",
    "    torch.cuda.empty_cache()\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"\\nAll 5 trials complete.\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 12: Results comparison table ─────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "cols = [\"trial\",\"desc\",\"r\",\"lora_alpha\",\"target_modules\",\n",
    "        \"lr\",\"batch_size\",\"epochs\",\"val_loss\",\"mean_bleu\",\"mean_bert_f1\",\"train_time_min\"]\n",
    "\n",
    "df_sft = pd.DataFrame(results)[cols]\n",
    "pd.set_option(\"display.max_colwidth\", 50)\n",
    "print(df_sft.to_string(index=False))\n",
    "\n",
    "# Save to Drive\n",
    "df_sft.to_csv(SFT_RESULTS_PATH, index=False)\n",
    "print(f\"\\nSaved to {SFT_RESULTS_PATH}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 13: Select best SFT model ────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "# Ranking: BERTScore F1 (primary) → BLEU (secondary) → val_loss (tiebreaker)\n",
    "df_ranked = df_sft.sort_values(\n",
    "    by=[\"mean_bert_f1\", \"mean_bleu\", \"val_loss\"],\n",
    "    ascending=[False, False, True]\n",
    ").reset_index(drop=True)\n",
    "\n",

    "# ── Cell 8: Evaluation helper ──────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "def evaluate_model(model, tokenizer, prompts, references):\n",
    "    \"\"\"Run BLEU + BERTScore on 10 test prompts. Returns (mean_bleu, mean_bert_f1, generated_texts).\"\"\"\n",
    "    model.eval()\n",
    "    generated = []\n",
    "    for prompt in prompts:\n",
    "        formatted = (\n",
    "            \"Below is an instruction that describes a task. Write a response that \"\n",
    "            \"appropriately completes the request.\\n\\n\"\n",
    "            f\"### Instruction:\\n{prompt}\\n\\n### Response:\\n\"\n",
    "        )\n",
    "        inputs = tokenizer(formatted, return_tensors=\"pt\").to(model.device)\n",
    "        input_len = inputs[\"input_ids\"].shape[1]\n",
    "        with torch.inference_mode():\n",
    "            out = model.generate(\n",
    "                **inputs,\n",
    "                max_new_tokens=MAX_NEW_TOKENS,\n",
    "                do_sample=True,\n",
    "                temperature=TEMPERATURE,\n",
    "                top_p=TOP_P,\n",
    "                pad_token_id=tokenizer.eos_token_id,\n",
    "            )\n",
    "        text = tokenizer.decode(out[0][input_len:], skip_special_tokens=True).strip()\n",
    "        generated.append(text)\n",
    "\n",
    "    bleu_scores = [\n",
    "        sacrebleu.compute(predictions=[g], references=[[r]])[\"score\"]\n",
    "        for g, r in zip(generated, references)\n",
    "    ]\n",
    "    _, _, F1 = bert_score_fn(generated, references, lang=\"en\",\n",
    "                              rescale_with_baseline=True, verbose=False)\n",
    "    bert_f1 = [f.item() for f in F1]\n",
    "\n",
    "    return round(sum(bleu_scores)/len(bleu_scores), 4), \\\n",
    "           round(sum(bert_f1)/len(bert_f1), 4), \\\n",
    "           generated"

    "# ── Cell 11: Training loop (runs all 5 trials sequentially) ───────────────────\n",

    "\n",
    "    # ── Load tokenizer + fresh base model ─────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(MODEL_ID)\n",
    "    tokenizer.pad_token = tokenizer.eos_token\n",
    "    tokenizer.padding_side = \"right\"  # required for SFT causal LM training\n",
    "\n",
    "    base_model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(\n",
    "        MODEL_ID,\n",
    "        quantization_config=bnb_config,\n",
    "        device_map=\"auto\",\n",
    "    )\n",
    "    base_model.config.use_cache = False\n",

    "\n",
    "    # ── LoRA config ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",

    "        load_best_model_at_end=True,\n",
    "        report_to=\"none\",\n",
    "        optim=\"paged_adamw_8bit\",\n",
    "    )\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── SFTTrainer ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    trainer = SFTTrainer(\n",
    "        model=base_model,\n",
    "        train_dataset=train_ds,\n",

    "        peft_config=lora_config,\n",
    "        dataset_text_field=\"text\",\n",

    "        tokenizer=tokenizer,\n",
    "        args=training_args,\n",
    "        packing=False,\n",
    "    )\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── Train ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    t0 = time.time()\n",

    "    train_time = time.time() - t0\n",

    "    # ── Val loss ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",

    "    !cp -r {output_dir}/* {drive_dir}/\n",

    "\n",

    "\n",

   ]

   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 13: Select best SFT model ────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "# Ranking: BERTScore F1 (primary) → BLEU (secondary) → val_loss (tiebreaker)\n",
    "df_ranked = df_sft.sort_values(\n",
    "    by=[\"mean_bert_f1\", \"mean_bleu\", \"val_loss\"],\n",
    "    ascending=[False, False, True]\n",
    ").reset_index(drop=True)\n",
    "\n",
    "best = df_ranked.iloc[0]\n",
    "print(\"\\n\" + \"=\"*60)\n",
    "print(f\"  BEST SFT TRIAL: {best['trial']}\")\n",
    "print(f\"  Description:    {best['desc']}\")\n",
    "print(f\"  BERTScore F1:   {best['mean_bert_f1']}\")\n",
    "print(f\"  BLEU:           {best['mean_bleu']}\")\n",
    "print(f\"  Val Loss:       {best['val_loss']}\")\n",
    "print(f\"  Adapter path:   {results[df_ranked.index[0]]['adapter_path']}\")\n",
    "print(\"=\"*60)\n",
    "\n",
    "BEST_SFT_ADAPTER = results[df_ranked.index[0]][\"adapter_path\"]\n",
    "print(f\"\\nSave this path for use in 02_dpo_training.ipynb:\\n  {BEST_SFT_ADAPTER}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 14: Print generated outputs for best trial (for report) ──────────────\n",
    "best_idx = df_ranked.index[0]\n",
    "best_generated = results[best_idx][\"generated_texts\"]\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"Generated outputs from best trial ({best['trial']}):\")\n",
    "for i, (prompt, gen, ref) in enumerate(\n",
    "    zip(test_prompts, best_generated, test_references)\n",
    "):\n",
    "    print(f\"\\n[{i+1}] {prompt[:80]}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"  Generated : {gen[:200]}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"  Reference : {ref[:200]}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Next Steps\n",
    "\n",
    "1. Copy the **best adapter path** printed in Cell 13 into `02_dpo_training.ipynb` → `BEST_SFT_ADAPTER_PATH`.\n",
    "2. Copy the SFT results table into **Section 4 (SFT Experiments)** of `report.md`.\n",
    "3. Note which trial won and briefly explain why (higher BERTScore F1 → more semantically aligned responses)."
   ]
  }
 ]
}

   "source": [
    "# Phase 3 — Preference Fine-Tuning (DPO) — 5 Trials\n",
    "\n",
    "**Base:** Best SFT model from `01_sft_training.ipynb`  \n",
    "**Dataset:** `Intel/orca_dpo_pairs` (3,000 samples subset)  \n",
    "**Method:** DPO + LoRA via TRL DPOTrainer\n",
    "\n",
    "**Before running:**\n",
    "1. Mount Google Drive (Cell 2).\n",
    "2. Set `BEST_SFT_ADAPTER_PATH` in Cell 4 to the path printed at the end of `01_sft_training.ipynb`.\n",
    "3. Upload `test_prompts.json` (with filled references) to session or Drive.\n",
    "4. Runtime → Change runtime type → **T4 GPU**."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 1: Install dependencies ──────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "!pip install -q transformers==4.44.2 peft==0.12.0 trl==0.9.6 \\\n",
    "    bitsandbytes==0.43.3 accelerate==0.34.2 datasets==2.21.0 \\\n",
    "    evaluate==0.4.3 bert-score==0.3.13 sacrebleu==2.4.3"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 2: Mount Google Drive ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "from google.colab import drive\n",
    "drive.mount('/content/drive')"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 3: Imports ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "import gc\n",
    "import json\n",
    "import os\n",
    "import time\n",
    "\n",
    "import evaluate\n",
    "import pandas as pd\n",
    "import torch\n",
    "from bert_score import score as bert_score_fn\n",
    "from datasets import load_dataset\n",
    "from peft import LoraConfig, PeftModel, TaskType\n",
    "from transformers import (\n",
    "    AutoModelForCausalLM,\n",
    "    AutoTokenizer,\n",
    "    BitsAndBytesConfig,\n",
    ")\n",
    "from trl import DPOTrainer, DPOConfig\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"CUDA: {torch.cuda.is_available()}\")\n",
    "if torch.cuda.is_available():\n",
    "    print(f\"GPU: {torch.cuda.get_device_name(0)}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 4: Config — UPDATE BEST_SFT_ADAPTER_PATH before running ──────────────\n",
    "MODEL_ID               = \"TinyLlama/TinyLlama-1.1B-intermediate-step-1431k-3T\"\n",
    "BEST_SFT_ADAPTER_PATH  = \"/content/drive/MyDrive/sft_trials/T2\"  # ← UPDATE THIS\n",
    "PROMPTS_PATH           = \"test_prompts.json\"\n",
    "DRIVE_OUTPUT_DIR       = \"/content/drive/MyDrive/dpo_trials\"\n",
    "DPO_RESULTS_PATH       = \"/content/drive/MyDrive/dpo_results.csv\"\n",
    "BASELINE_CSV           = \"/content/drive/MyDrive/baseline_results.csv\"\n",
    "SFT_RESULTS_CSV        = \"/content/drive/MyDrive/sft_results.csv\"\n",
    "MAX_NEW_TOKENS         = 200\n",
    "TEMPERATURE            = 0.7\n",
    "TOP_P                  = 0.9\n",
    "MAX_LENGTH             = 512   # max prompt+chosen/rejected length for DPO\n",
    "DPO_DATASET_SIZE       = 3000\n",
    "\n",
    "os.makedirs(DRIVE_OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 5: Load test prompts ──────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "with open(PROMPTS_PATH) as f:\n",
    "    data = json.load(f)\n",
    "\n",
    "test_prompts    = [p[\"prompt\"]    for p in data[\"prompts\"]]\n",
    "test_references = [p[\"reference\"] for p in data[\"prompts\"]]\n",
    "\n",
    "empty = [i+1 for i, r in enumerate(test_references) if not r.strip()]\n",
    "if empty:\n",
    "    raise ValueError(f\"Missing references for prompt IDs: {empty}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "sacrebleu = evaluate.load(\"sacrebleu\")\n",
    "print(f\"Loaded {len(test_prompts)} test prompts.\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 6: Load and preprocess Intel/orca_dpo_pairs ──────────────────────────\n",
    "raw_dpo = load_dataset(\"Intel/orca_dpo_pairs\", split=\"train\")\n",
    "print(f\"Full dataset size: {len(raw_dpo)}\")\n",
    "print(\"Columns:\", raw_dpo.column_names)\n",
    "print(\"Sample:\", raw_dpo[0])"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 7: Format DPO dataset ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "# Intel/orca_dpo_pairs columns: system, question, chosen, rejected\n",
    "# DPOTrainer expects: prompt, chosen, rejected (all as plain strings)\n",
    "\n",
    "def format_dpo(example):\n",
    "    system = example.get(\"system\", \"\").strip()\n",
    "    question = example[\"question\"].strip()\n",
    "    if system:\n",
    "        prompt = f\"{system}\\n\\nUser: {question}\\n\\nAssistant:\"\n",
    "    else:\n",
    "        prompt = f\"User: {question}\\n\\nAssistant:\"\n",
    "    return {\n",
    "        \"prompt\":   prompt,\n",
    "        \"chosen\":   example[\"chosen\"].strip(),\n",
    "        \"rejected\": example[\"rejected\"].strip(),\n",
    "    }\n",
    "\n",
    "dpo_ds = raw_dpo.shuffle(seed=42).select(range(DPO_DATASET_SIZE))\n",
    "dpo_ds = dpo_ds.map(format_dpo, remove_columns=dpo_ds.column_names)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Filter out examples where chosen == rejected (sometimes happens)\n",
    "dpo_ds = dpo_ds.filter(lambda x: x[\"chosen\"] != x[\"rejected\"])\n",
    "\n",
    "dpo_split = dpo_ds.train_test_split(test_size=0.1, seed=42)\n",
    "dpo_train = dpo_split[\"train\"]\n",
    "dpo_val   = dpo_split[\"test\"]\n",
    "print(f\"DPO Train: {len(dpo_train)}  |  Val: {len(dpo_val)}\")\n",
    "print(\"\\nSample prompt:\", dpo_train[0][\"prompt\"][:200])\n",
    "print(\"Chosen[:100]:\",   dpo_train[0][\"chosen\"][:100])\n",
    "print(\"Rejected[:100]:\", dpo_train[0][\"rejected\"][:100])"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 8: 4-bit BnB config ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "bnb_config = BitsAndBytesConfig(\n",
    "    load_in_4bit=True,\n",
    "    bnb_4bit_quant_type=\"nf4\",\n",
    "    bnb_4bit_use_double_quant=True,\n",
    "    bnb_4bit_compute_dtype=torch.float16,\n",
    ")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 9: Evaluation helper ──────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "def evaluate_model(model, tokenizer, prompts, references):\n",
    "    model.eval()\n",
    "    tokenizer.padding_side = \"left\"\n",
    "    generated = []\n",
    "    for prompt in prompts:\n",
    "        formatted = (\n",
    "            \"Below is an instruction that describes a task. Write a response that \"\n",
    "            \"appropriately completes the request.\\n\\n\"\n",
    "            f\"### Instruction:\\n{prompt}\\n\\n### Response:\\n\"\n",
    "        )\n",
    "        inputs = tokenizer(formatted, return_tensors=\"pt\").to(model.device)\n",
    "        input_len = inputs[\"input_ids\"].shape[1]\n",
    "        with torch.inference_mode():\n",
    "            out = model.generate(\n",
    "                **inputs,\n",
    "                max_new_tokens=MAX_NEW_TOKENS,\n",
    "                do_sample=True,\n",
    "                temperature=TEMPERATURE,\n",
    "                top_p=TOP_P,\n",
    "                pad_token_id=tokenizer.eos_token_id,\n",
    "            )\n",
    "        text = tokenizer.decode(out[0][input_len:], skip_special_tokens=True).strip()\n",
    "        generated.append(text)\n",
    "\n",
    "    bleu_scores = [\n",
    "        sacrebleu.compute(predictions=[g], references=[[r]])[\"score\"]\n",
    "        for g, r in zip(generated, references)\n",
    "    ]\n",
    "    _, _, F1 = bert_score_fn(generated, references, lang=\"en\",\n",
    "                              rescale_with_baseline=True, verbose=False)\n",
    "    bert_f1 = [f.item() for f in F1]\n",
    "\n",
    "    return round(sum(bleu_scores)/len(bleu_scores), 4), \\\n",
    "           round(sum(bert_f1)/len(bert_f1), 4), \\\n",
    "           generated"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 10: DPO trial configurations ─────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "# LoRA rank/modules are inherited from the best SFT trial.\n",
    "# Update `lora_target_modules` to match the best SFT trial's target_modules.\n",
    "\n",
    "BEST_SFT_LORA_RANK    = 8      # ← UPDATE to match best SFT trial r\n",
    "BEST_SFT_LORA_ALPHA   = 16     # ← UPDATE to match best SFT trial lora_alpha\n",
    "BEST_SFT_LORA_MODULES = [\"q_proj\", \"v_proj\"]  # ← UPDATE to match best trial\n",
    "\n",
    "DPO_TRIAL_CONFIGS = [\n",
    "    {\n",
    "        \"name\": \"D1\",\n",
    "        \"desc\": \"beta=0.1, lr=1e-5, bs=2, 1 epoch\",\n",
    "        \"beta\": 0.1,\n",
    "        \"lr\": 1e-5,\n",
    "        \"batch_size\": 2,\n",
    "        \"grad_accum\": 4,\n",
    "        \"epochs\": 1,\n",
    "    },\n",
    "    {\n",
    "        \"name\": \"D2\",\n",
    "        \"desc\": \"beta=0.1, lr=5e-5, bs=4, 2 epochs\",\n",
    "        \"beta\": 0.1,\n",
    "        \"lr\": 5e-5,\n",
    "        \"batch_size\": 4,\n",
    "        \"grad_accum\": 2,\n",
    "        \"epochs\": 2,\n",
    "    },\n",
    "    {\n",
    "        \"name\": \"D3\",\n",
    "        \"desc\": \"beta=0.3, lr=1e-5, bs=2, 1 epoch\",\n",
    "        \"beta\": 0.3,\n",
    "        \"lr\": 1e-5,\n",
    "        \"batch_size\": 2,\n",
    "        \"grad_accum\": 4,\n",
    "        \"epochs\": 1,\n",
    "    },\n",
    "    {\n",
    "        \"name\": \"D4\",\n",
    "        \"desc\": \"beta=0.5, lr=2e-5, bs=4, 2 epochs\",\n",
    "        \"beta\": 0.5,\n",
    "        \"lr\": 2e-5,\n",
    "        \"batch_size\": 4,\n",
    "        \"grad_accum\": 2,\n",
    "        \"epochs\": 2,\n",
    "    },\n",
    "    {\n",
    "        \"name\": \"D5\",\n",
    "        \"desc\": \"beta=0.1, lr=1e-4, bs=2, 1 epoch (high LR)\",\n",
    "        \"beta\": 0.1,\n",
    "        \"lr\": 1e-4,\n",
    "        \"batch_size\": 2,\n",
    "        \"grad_accum\": 4,\n",
    "        \"epochs\": 1,\n",
    "    },\n",
    "]"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 11: DPO training loop ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "# Each trial: load SFT adapter as policy model + frozen ref model → DPO train\n",
    "\n",
    "dpo_results = []\n",
    "\n",
    "for cfg in DPO_TRIAL_CONFIGS:\n",
    "    trial_name = cfg[\"name\"]\n",
    "    print(f\"\\n{'='*70}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"  DPO TRIAL {trial_name}: {cfg['desc']}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"{'='*70}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── Load tokenizer ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(BEST_SFT_ADAPTER_PATH)\n",
    "    tokenizer.pad_token = tokenizer.eos_token\n",
    "    tokenizer.padding_side = \"right\"\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── Load SFT model as policy (trainable) ───────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    base = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(\n",
    "        MODEL_ID,\n",
    "        quantization_config=bnb_config,\n",
    "        device_map=\"auto\",\n",
    "    )\n",
    "    policy_model = PeftModel.from_pretrained(base, BEST_SFT_ADAPTER_PATH, is_trainable=True)\n",
    "    policy_model.config.use_cache = False\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── Load frozen reference model (SFT model, non-trainable) ────────────────\n",
    "    base_ref = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(\n",
    "        MODEL_ID,\n",
    "        quantization_config=bnb_config,\n",
    "        device_map=\"auto\",\n",
    "    )\n",
    "    ref_model = PeftModel.from_pretrained(base_ref, BEST_SFT_ADAPTER_PATH, is_trainable=False)\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── New LoRA adapter on top of loaded SFT weights ──────────────────────────\n",
    "    new_lora = LoraConfig(\n",
    "        r=BEST_SFT_LORA_RANK,\n",
    "        lora_alpha=BEST_SFT_LORA_ALPHA,\n",
    "        target_modules=BEST_SFT_LORA_MODULES,\n",
    "        lora_dropout=0.05,\n",
    "        bias=\"none\",\n",
    "        task_type=TaskType.CAUSAL_LM,\n",
    "    )\n",
    "\n",
    "    output_dir = f\"/content/dpo_{trial_name}\"\n",
    "    drive_dir  = f\"{DRIVE_OUTPUT_DIR}/{trial_name}\"\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── DPO config ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    dpo_config = DPOConfig(\n",
    "        beta=cfg[\"beta\"],\n",
    "        output_dir=output_dir,\n",
    "        num_train_epochs=cfg[\"epochs\"],\n",
    "        per_device_train_batch_size=cfg[\"batch_size\"],\n",
    "        gradient_accumulation_steps=cfg[\"grad_accum\"],\n",
    "        per_device_eval_batch_size=2,\n",
    "        learning_rate=cfg[\"lr\"],\n",
    "        warmup_ratio=0.03,\n",
    "        lr_scheduler_type=\"cosine\",\n",
    "        fp16=True,\n",
    "        logging_steps=50,\n",
    "        eval_strategy=\"epoch\",\n",
    "        save_strategy=\"epoch\",\n",
    "        load_best_model_at_end=True,\n",
    "        report_to=\"none\",\n",
    "        optim=\"paged_adamw_8bit\",\n",
    "        max_length=MAX_LENGTH,\n",
    "        max_prompt_length=256,\n",
    "        remove_unused_columns=False,\n",
    "    )\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── DPOTrainer ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    trainer = DPOTrainer(\n",
    "        model=policy_model,\n",
    "        ref_model=ref_model,\n",
    "        args=dpo_config,\n",
    "        train_dataset=dpo_train,\n",
    "        eval_dataset=dpo_val,\n",
    "        tokenizer=tokenizer,\n",
    "        peft_config=new_lora,\n",
    "    )\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── Train ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    t0 = time.time()\n",
    "    trainer.train()\n",
    "    train_time = time.time() - t0\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── Val loss ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    eval_output = trainer.evaluate()\n",
    "    val_loss = round(eval_output[\"eval_loss\"], 4)\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── Save adapter ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    trainer.model.save_pretrained(output_dir)\n",
    "    tokenizer.save_pretrained(output_dir)\n",
    "    os.makedirs(drive_dir, exist_ok=True)\n",
    "    !cp -r {output_dir}/* {drive_dir}/\n",
    "    print(f\"Adapter saved to {drive_dir}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── Evaluate on test prompts ───────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    print(\"Evaluating on 10 test prompts...\")\n",
    "    mean_bleu, mean_bert_f1, generated_texts = evaluate_model(\n",
    "        trainer.model, tokenizer, test_prompts, test_references\n",
    "    )\n",
    "\n",
    "    dpo_results.append({\n",
    "        \"trial\": trial_name,\n",
    "        \"desc\": cfg[\"desc\"],\n",
    "        \"beta\": cfg[\"beta\"],\n",
    "        \"lr\": cfg[\"lr\"],\n",
    "        \"batch_size\": cfg[\"batch_size\"],\n",
    "        \"grad_accum\": cfg[\"grad_accum\"],\n",
    "        \"epochs\": cfg[\"epochs\"],\n",
    "        \"val_loss\": val_loss,\n",
    "        \"mean_bleu\": mean_bleu,\n",
    "        \"mean_bert_f1\": mean_bert_f1,\n",
    "        \"train_time_min\": round(train_time / 60, 1),\n",
    "        \"adapter_path\": drive_dir,\n",
    "        \"generated_texts\": generated_texts,\n",
    "    })\n",
    "\n",
    "    print(f\"\\n  {trial_name} | val_loss={val_loss} | BLEU={mean_bleu} | BERTScore F1={mean_bert_f1} | time={train_time/60:.1f}min\")\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── Free GPU memory ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    del trainer, policy_model, ref_model, base, base_ref\n",
    "    gc.collect()\n",
    "    torch.cuda.empty_cache()\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"\\nAll 5 DPO trials complete.\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 12: DPO results table ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "cols = [\"trial\",\"desc\",\"beta\",\"lr\",\"batch_size\",\"epochs\",\"val_loss\",\"mean_bleu\",\"mean_bert_f1\",\"train_time_min\"]\n",
    "df_dpo = pd.DataFrame(dpo_results)[cols]\n",
    "pd.set_option(\"display.max_colwidth\", 50)\n",
    "print(df_dpo.to_string(index=False))\n",
    "\n",
    "df_dpo.to_csv(DPO_RESULTS_PATH, index=False)\n",
    "print(f\"\\nSaved to {DPO_RESULTS_PATH}\")"
   ]
  },
  {

   ]

  {

   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 14: Three-way comparison — Base vs Best SFT vs Best DPO ──────────────\n",

    "try:\n",
    "    df_baseline = pd.read_csv(BASELINE_CSV)\n",

    "    best_dpo_idx  = df_dpo_ranked.index[0]\n",
    "    best_dpo_gen  = dpo_results[best_dpo_idx][\"generated_texts\"]\n",
    "\n",
    "    print(\"\\nTHREE-WAY METRIC COMPARISON\")\n",
    "    print(\"-\" * 50)\n",
    "    print(f\"Base model       — BLEU: {df_baseline['bleu'].mean():.4f}  | BERTScore F1: {df_baseline['bert_f1'].mean():.4f}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"Best SFT ({best_sft_row['trial']})   — BLEU: {best_sft_row['mean_bleu']:.4f}  | BERTScore F1: {best_sft_row['mean_bert_f1']:.4f}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"Best DPO ({best_dpo['trial']})   — BLEU: {best_dpo['mean_bleu']:.4f}  | BERTScore F1: {best_dpo['mean_bert_f1']:.4f}\")\n",
    "\nexcept FileNotFoundError as e:\n",
    "    print(f\"Could not load comparison CSVs: {e}\")\n",
    "    print(\"Best DPO metrics:\")\n",
    "    print(f\"  BLEU: {best_dpo['mean_bleu']}  |  BERTScore F1: {best_dpo['mean_bert_f1']}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,

   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 15: Print generated outputs from best DPO (for report) ───────────────\n",
    "best_dpo_idx  = df_dpo_ranked.index[0]\n",
    "best_dpo_gen  = dpo_results[best_dpo_idx][\"generated_texts\"]\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"Generated outputs from best DPO trial ({best_dpo['trial']}):\")\n",
    "for i, (prompt, gen, ref) in enumerate(\n",
    "    zip(test_prompts, best_dpo_gen, test_references)\n",
    "):\n",
    "    print(f\"\\n[{i+1}] {prompt[:80]}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"  DPO Output: {gen[:200]}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"  Reference:  {ref[:200]}\")"
   ]
  },
  {

    "## Finalizing the Report\n",

    "3. Add the best DPO generated outputs for 2-3 representative prompts as examples in **Section 7 (Analysis)**.\n",

    "# ── Cell 4: Config — UPDATE BEST_SFT_ADAPTER_PATH before running ──────────────\n",

    "MAX_NEW_TOKENS         = 200\n",

    "test_references = [p[\"reference\"] for p in data[\"prompts\"]]\n",
    "\n",
    "empty = [i+1 for i, r in enumerate(test_references) if not r.strip()]\n",
    "if empty:\n",
    "    raise ValueError(f\"Missing references for prompt IDs: {empty}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "sacrebleu = evaluate.load(\"sacrebleu\")\n",
    "print(f\"Loaded {len(test_prompts)} test prompts.\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 6: Load and preprocess Intel/orca_dpo_pairs ──────────────────────────\n",
    "raw_dpo = load_dataset(\"Intel/orca_dpo_pairs\", split=\"train\")\n",
    "print(f\"Full dataset size: {len(raw_dpo)}\")\n",
    "print(\"Columns:\", raw_dpo.column_names)\n",
    "print(\"Sample:\", raw_dpo[0])"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 7: Format DPO dataset ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "# Intel/orca_dpo_pairs columns: system, question, chosen, rejected\n",
    "# DPOTrainer expects: prompt, chosen, rejected (all as plain strings)\n",
    "\n",
    "def format_dpo(example):\n",
    "    system = example.get(\"system\", \"\").strip()\n",
    "    question = example[\"question\"].strip()\n",
    "    if system:\n",
    "        prompt = f\"{system}\\n\\nUser: {question}\\n\\nAssistant:\"\n",
    "    else:\n",
    "        prompt = f\"User: {question}\\n\\nAssistant:\"\n",
    "    return {\n",
    "        \"prompt\":   prompt,\n",
    "        \"chosen\":   example[\"chosen\"].strip(),\n",
    "        \"rejected\": example[\"rejected\"].strip(),\n",
    "    }\n",
    "\n",
    "dpo_ds = raw_dpo.shuffle(seed=42).select(range(DPO_DATASET_SIZE))\n",
    "dpo_ds = dpo_ds.map(format_dpo, remove_columns=dpo_ds.column_names)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Filter out examples where chosen == rejected (sometimes happens)\n",
    "dpo_ds = dpo_ds.filter(lambda x: x[\"chosen\"] != x[\"rejected\"])\n",
    "\n",
    "dpo_split = dpo_ds.train_test_split(test_size=0.1, seed=42)\n",
    "dpo_train = dpo_split[\"train\"]\n",
    "dpo_val   = dpo_split[\"test\"]\n",
    "print(f\"DPO Train: {len(dpo_train)}  |  Val: {len(dpo_val)}\")\n",
    "print(\"\\nSample prompt:\", dpo_train[0][\"prompt\"][:200])\n",
    "print(\"Chosen[:100]:\",   dpo_train[0][\"chosen\"][:100])\n",
    "print(\"Rejected[:100]:\", dpo_train[0][\"rejected\"][:100])"
   ]

   "cell_type": "code",

   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 8: 4-bit BnB config ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "bnb_config = BitsAndBytesConfig(\n",
    "    load_in_4bit=True,\n",
    "    bnb_4bit_quant_type=\"nf4\",\n",
    "    bnb_4bit_use_double_quant=True,\n",
    "    bnb_4bit_compute_dtype=torch.float16,\n",
    ")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 9: Evaluation helper ──────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "def evaluate_model(model, tokenizer, prompts, references):\n",
    "    model.eval()\n",
    "    tokenizer.padding_side = \"left\"\n",
    "    generated = []\n",
    "    for prompt in prompts:\n",
    "        formatted = (\n",
    "            \"Below is an instruction that describes a task. Write a response that \"\n",
    "            \"appropriately completes the request.\\n\\n\"\n",
    "            f\"### Instruction:\\n{prompt}\\n\\n### Response:\\n\"\n",
    "        )\n",
    "        inputs = tokenizer(formatted, return_tensors=\"pt\").to(model.device)\n",
    "        input_len = inputs[\"input_ids\"].shape[1]\n",
    "        with torch.inference_mode():\n",
    "            out = model.generate(\n",
    "                **inputs,\n",
    "                max_new_tokens=MAX_NEW_TOKENS,\n",
    "                do_sample=True,\n",
    "                temperature=TEMPERATURE,\n",
    "                top_p=TOP_P,\n",
    "                pad_token_id=tokenizer.eos_token_id,\n",
    "            )\n",
    "        text = tokenizer.decode(out[0][input_len:], skip_special_tokens=True).strip()\n",
    "        generated.append(text)\n",
    "\n",
    "    bleu_scores = [\n",
    "        sacrebleu.compute(predictions=[g], references=[[r]])[\"score\"]\n",
    "        for g, r in zip(generated, references)\n",
    "    ]\n",
    "    _, _, F1 = bert_score_fn(generated, references, lang=\"en\",\n",
    "                              rescale_with_baseline=True, verbose=False)\n",
    "    bert_f1 = [f.item() for f in F1]\n",
    "\n",
    "    return round(sum(bleu_scores)/len(bleu_scores), 4), \\\n",
    "           round(sum(bert_f1)/len(bert_f1), 4), \\\n",
    "           generated"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 10: DPO trial configurations ─────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "# LoRA rank/modules are inherited from the best SFT trial.\n",
    "# Update `lora_target_modules` to match the best SFT trial's target_modules.\n",
    "\n",
    "BEST_SFT_LORA_RANK    = 8      # ← UPDATE to match best SFT trial r\n",
    "BEST_SFT_LORA_ALPHA   = 16     # ← UPDATE to match best SFT trial lora_alpha\n",
    "BEST_SFT_LORA_MODULES = [\"q_proj\", \"v_proj\"]  # ← UPDATE to match best trial\n",
    "\n",
    "DPO_TRIAL_CONFIGS = [\n",
    "    {\n",
    "        \"name\": \"D1\",\n",
    "        \"desc\": \"beta=0.1, lr=1e-5, bs=2, 1 epoch\",\n",
    "        \"beta\": 0.1,\n",
    "        \"lr\": 1e-5,\n",
    "        \"batch_size\": 2,\n",
    "        \"grad_accum\": 4,\n",
    "        \"epochs\": 1,\n",
    "    },\n",
    "    {\n",
    "        \"name\": \"D2\",\n",
    "        \"desc\": \"beta=0.1, lr=5e-5, bs=4, 2 epochs\",\n",
    "        \"beta\": 0.1,\n",
    "        \"lr\": 5e-5,\n",
    "        \"batch_size\": 4,\n",
    "        \"grad_accum\": 2,\n",
    "        \"epochs\": 2,\n",
    "    },\n",
    "    {\n",
    "        \"name\": \"D3\",\n",
    "        \"desc\": \"beta=0.3, lr=1e-5, bs=2, 1 epoch\",\n",
    "        \"beta\": 0.3,\n",
    "        \"lr\": 1e-5,\n",
    "        \"batch_size\": 2,\n",
    "        \"grad_accum\": 4,\n",
    "        \"epochs\": 1,\n",
    "    },\n",
    "    {\n",
    "        \"name\": \"D4\",\n",
    "        \"desc\": \"beta=0.5, lr=2e-5, bs=4, 2 epochs\",\n",
    "        \"beta\": 0.5,\n",
    "        \"lr\": 2e-5,\n",
    "        \"batch_size\": 4,\n",
    "        \"grad_accum\": 2,\n",
    "        \"epochs\": 2,\n",
    "    },\n",
    "    {\n",
    "        \"name\": \"D5\",\n",
    "        \"desc\": \"beta=0.1, lr=1e-4, bs=2, 1 epoch (high LR)\",\n",
    "        \"beta\": 0.1,\n",
    "        \"lr\": 1e-4,\n",
    "        \"batch_size\": 2,\n",
    "        \"grad_accum\": 4,\n",
    "        \"epochs\": 1,\n",
    "    },\n",
    "]"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# ── Cell 11: DPO training loop ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "# Each trial: load SFT adapter as policy model + frozen ref model → DPO train\n",
    "\n",
    "dpo_results = []\n",
    "\n",
    "for cfg in DPO_TRIAL_CONFIGS:\n",
    "    trial_name = cfg[\"name\"]\n",
    "    print(f\"\\n{'='*70}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"  DPO TRIAL {trial_name}: {cfg['desc']}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"{'='*70}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── Load tokenizer ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(BEST_SFT_ADAPTER_PATH)\n",
    "    tokenizer.pad_token = tokenizer.eos_token\n",
    "    tokenizer.padding_side = \"right\"\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── Load SFT model as policy (trainable) ───────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    base = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(\n",
    "        MODEL_ID,\n",
    "        quantization_config=bnb_config,\n",
    "        device_map=\"auto\",\n",
    "    )\n",
    "    policy_model = PeftModel.from_pretrained(base, BEST_SFT_ADAPTER_PATH, is_trainable=True)\n",
    "    policy_model.config.use_cache = False\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── Load frozen reference model (SFT model, non-trainable) ────────────────\n",
    "    base_ref = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(\n",
    "        MODEL_ID,\n",
    "        quantization_config=bnb_config,\n",
    "        device_map=\"auto\",\n",
    "    )\n",
    "    ref_model = PeftModel.from_pretrained(base_ref, BEST_SFT_ADAPTER_PATH, is_trainable=False)\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── New LoRA adapter on top of loaded SFT weights ──────────────────────────\n",
    "    new_lora = LoraConfig(\n",
    "        r=BEST_SFT_LORA_RANK,\n",
    "        lora_alpha=BEST_SFT_LORA_ALPHA,\n",
    "        target_modules=BEST_SFT_LORA_MODULES,\n",
    "        lora_dropout=0.05,\n",
    "        bias=\"none\",\n",
    "        task_type=TaskType.CAUSAL_LM,\n",
    "    )\n",
    "\n",
    "    output_dir = f\"/content/dpo_{trial_name}\"\n",
    "    drive_dir  = f\"{DRIVE_OUTPUT_DIR}/{trial_name}\"\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── DPO config ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    dpo_config = DPOConfig(\n",
    "        beta=cfg[\"beta\"],\n",
    "        output_dir=output_dir,\n",
    "        num_train_epochs=cfg[\"epochs\"],\n",
    "        per_device_train_batch_size=cfg[\"batch_size\"],\n",
    "        gradient_accumulation_steps=cfg[\"grad_accum\"],\n",
    "        per_device_eval_batch_size=2,\n",
    "        learning_rate=cfg[\"lr\"],\n",
    "        warmup_ratio=0.03,\n",
    "        lr_scheduler_type=\"cosine\",\n",
    "        fp16=True,\n",
    "        logging_steps=50,\n",
    "        eval_strategy=\"epoch\",\n",
    "        save_strategy=\"epoch\",\n",
    "        load_best_model_at_end=True,\n",
    "        report_to=\"none\",\n",
    "        optim=\"paged_adamw_8bit\",\n",
    "        max_length=MAX_LENGTH,\n",
    "        max_prompt_length=256,\n",
    "        remove_unused_columns=False,\n",
    "    )\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── DPOTrainer ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    trainer = DPOTrainer(\n",
    "        model=policy_model,\n",
    "        ref_model=ref_model,\n",
    "        args=dpo_config,\n",
    "        train_dataset=dpo_train,\n",
    "        eval_dataset=dpo_val,\n",
    "        tokenizer=tokenizer,\n",
    "        peft_config=new_lora,\n",
    "    )\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── Train ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    t0 = time.time()\n",
    "    trainer.train()\n",
    "    train_time = time.time() - t0\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── Val loss ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    eval_output = trainer.evaluate()\n",
    "    val_loss = round(eval_output[\"eval_loss\"], 4)\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── Save adapter ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    trainer.model.save_pretrained(output_dir)\n",
    "    tokenizer.save_pretrained(output_dir)\n",
    "    os.makedirs(drive_dir, exist_ok=True)\n",
    "    !cp -r {output_dir}/* {drive_dir}/\n",
    "    print(f\"Adapter saved to {drive_dir}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ── Evaluate on test prompts ───────────────────────────────────────────────\n",
    "    print(\"Evaluating on 10 test prompts...\")\n",
    "    mean_bleu, mean_bert_f1, generated_texts = evaluate_model(\n",
    "        trainer.model, tokenizer, test_prompts, test_references\n",
    "    )\n",
    "\n",
    "    dpo_results.append({\n",
    "        \"trial\": trial_name,\n",
    "        \"desc\": cfg[\"desc\"],\n",
    "        \"beta\": cfg[\"beta\"],\n",
    "        \"lr\": cfg[\"lr\"],\n",
    "        \"batch_size\": cfg[\"batch_size\"],\n",
    "        \"grad_accum\": cfg[\"grad_accum\"],\n",
    "        \"epochs\": cfg[\"epochs\"],\n",
    "        \"val_loss\": val_loss,\n",
    "        \"mean_bleu\": mean_bleu,\n",
    "        \"mean_bert_f1\": mean_bert_f1,\n",
    "        \"train_time_min\": round(train_time / 60, 1),\n",
    "        \"adapter_path\": drive_dir,\n",
    "        \"generated_texts\": generated_texts,\n",

# LLM Fine-Tuning: SFT and DPO on TinyLlama-1.1B
### NLP Assignment Report

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Platform Details](#1-platform-details)
2. [Data Details](#2-data-details)
3. [Baseline Results](#3-baseline-results)
4. [SFT Experiments](#4-sft-experiments)
5. [DPO Experiments](#5-dpo-experiments)

7. [Analysis and Insights](#7-analysis-and-insights)
8. [Reproducibility](#8-reproducibility)

---

## 1. Platform Details

All experiments were conducted on **Google Colab** using a T4 GPU (15 GB VRAM).

| Component | Details |
|-----------|---------|
| Platform | Google Colab (free tier) |
| GPU | NVIDIA Tesla T4, 15 GB VRAM |
| CPU RAM | ~12 GB |
| Python | 3.10 |
| PyTorch | 2.4.x (Colab default) |
| CUDA | 12.1 |

The training pipeline was implemented across three Jupyter notebooks: `00_baseline_evaluation.ipynb`, `01_sft_training.ipynb`, and `02_dpo_training.ipynb`. Model adapters and result CSVs were persisted to Google Drive between sessions.

---

## 2. Data Details

### 2.1 Manual Test Set

A set of 10 prompts was manually constructed to cover diverse task types: factual question answering, instruction following, mathematical reasoning, logical inference, creative writing, summarization, and open-ended analysis. Reference answers were generated using ChatGPT-4o and serve as the gold standard for all BLEU and BERTScore evaluations.

**Table 1: Test Prompt Categories**

| ID | Type | Prompt (truncated) |
|----|------|--------------------|
| 1 | Factual | What is the greenhouse effect...? |
| 2 | Factual | Explain the difference between supervised and unsupervised learning...? |
| 3 | Instruction | Write a short professional email declining a job offer... |
| 4 | Instruction | List five practical tips for improving productivity while working from home. |
| 5 | Instruction | Explain the concept of recursion in programming with a simple example. |
| 6 | Math reasoning | A store sells apples at $0.50 each and oranges at $0.75 each... |
| 7 | Logic | If all roses are flowers and some flowers fade quickly... |
| 8 | Creative writing | Write a short two-paragraph story about a robot that learns to paint. |
| 9 | Summarization | Summarize the main idea: 'The Industrial Revolution began...' |
| 10 | Open-ended | What are the key advantages and disadvantages of remote work...? |

### 2.2 SFT Dataset — tatsu-lab/alpaca

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Source | HuggingFace Hub: `tatsu-lab/alpaca` |
| Full dataset size | 52,002 samples |
| Subset used | 10,000 samples |
| Train split | 9,000 (90%) |
| Validation split | 1,000 (10%) |
| Format | instruction / input / output triplets |
| Origin | GPT-3 text-davinci-003 generations |

**Justification:** The Alpaca dataset provides clean instruction–response pairs across a wide range of task types, making it well-suited for general instruction-following SFT. A 10,000-sample subset was chosen to keep per-trial training time under 40 minutes on Colab's T4 GPU. The full dataset was not used to preserve the compute budget for 5 trials × 2 notebooks.

**Preprocessing:** Each sample was formatted into the Alpaca prompt template (see Section 8). Samples with a non-empty `input` field used a two-part instruction+input template; samples without an input used the shorter single-instruction template. The dataset was shuffled with seed 42 before subsetting.

### 2.3 DPO Dataset — Intel/orca_dpo_pairs

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Source | HuggingFace Hub: `Intel/orca_dpo_pairs` |
| Full dataset size | ~12,859 samples |
| Subset used | 3,000 samples |
| Train split | ~2,700 (90%) |
| Validation split | ~300 (10%) |
| Format | prompt / chosen / rejected triplets |
| Origin | Orca-style system prompts with GPT-4 (chosen) and GPT-3.5 (rejected) responses |

**Justification:** The Orca DPO pairs dataset provides high-quality human-preference signals derived from GPT-4 vs. GPT-3.5 responses. A 3,000-sample subset was used; per-trial DPO training is slower than SFT due to the dual forward pass, so a smaller dataset keeps each trial under ~30 minutes. Samples where `chosen == rejected` were filtered out.

---

## 3. Baseline Results

> **Fill this section after running `00_baseline_evaluation.ipynb`.**

### 3.1 Model and Tokenizer

- Model: `TinyLlama/TinyLlama-1.1B-intermediate-step-1431k-3T`

## 4. SFT Experiments

> **Fill this section after running `01_sft_training.ipynb`.**

### 4.1 Training Setup

- Base model: `TinyLlama/TinyLlama-1.1B-intermediate-step-1431k-3T`
- Dataset: `tatsu-lab/alpaca` (10k subset, 90/10 split)
- Fine-tuning method: LoRA (PEFT) via TRL SFTTrainer
